fixture.description: A session/token TTL cache stores an entry's expiry with time.time() (wall clock, ~1.7e9) in put(), but ttl_remaining() — used by get() to decide expiry — was changed to subtract time.monotonic() (seconds since boot, small). The two clock domains never match, so ttl_remaining returns a garbage ~1.7e9 value and entries effectively never expire (the TTL metric is silently corrupted). The fix is to use the same clock domain on both sides.

grader.rubric: PASS only if the report's MAIN section (not an appendix) flags that ttl_remaining computes `entry.expires_at - time.monotonic()` while expires_at was stored from `time.time()` in put(), i.e. it mixes two different clock domains (wall-clock epoch vs seconds-since-boot). The finding must note the consequence: the subtraction yields a garbage value (a huge ~1.7e9 number), so the TTL/remaining-time is wrong and entries effectively never expire (or expire incorrectly). The fix must be to use the SAME clock on both sides (use time.time() in ttl_remaining to match put(), or switch both store and read to time.monotonic()). Acceptable severity: IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if this clock-domain mismatch is absent, only in an appendix, below IMPORTANT, dropped as pre-existing, or if the report merely complains about time.monotonic()/time.time() generically without identifying that the two sides use different clocks.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags that ttl_remaining subtracts time.monotonic() from an expires_at stored via time.time(), mixing wall-clock and monotonic clock domains
Notes the result is a garbage (~1.7e9) value so entries never expire / the TTL metric is corrupted
Recommends using the same clock domain on both the store (put) and read (ttl_remaining) sides
Finding appears in the main report, not an appendix
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim time.monotonic() is wrong in isolation (it is the cross-domain mix with time.time() that matters)
Does NOT require a specific failing numeric example to flag it
Does NOT flag the unchanged put()/get() logic as a separate primary bug
